180		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
181		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
182		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
183		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
184		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
185		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
186		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
187		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
188		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
189		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
190		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
191		// occurring.
192		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
193	}
194	
195	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
196		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
197	}
198	
199	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
200	
201	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
202		return f(ctx, beadID)
203	}
204	
205	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
206	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
207	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
208	// the current attempt.
209	//
210	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
211	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
212	// leaves the bead open.
213	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
214		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
215	}
216	
217	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
218	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
219	
220	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
221		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
222	}
223	
224	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
225		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
226		Claim(id, assignee string) error
227		Unclaim(id string) error
228		Heartbeat(id string) error
229		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
230		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
231		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
232		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
233		AppendNotes(id string, notes string) error
234		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
235		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
236		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
237		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.
238		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
239		// Update mutates a bead in place. Used by the post-merge triage step to
240		// add labels (e.g. "needs_human") and metadata hints (e.g. tier-pin) when
241		// the triage policy escalates after repeated review BLOCKs.
242		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
243	}
244	
245	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
246	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
247	type readyDiagnoser interface {
248		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
249	}
250	
251	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
252	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
253	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
254	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
255		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
256		SkippedOnCooldown  []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
257		SkippedNotEligible []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
258		SkippedSuperseded  []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
259		NextRetryAfter     string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`

data.text:
Committed `4d5cc5a`. AC verification:

1. ✅ `cli/internal/agent/try/outcome.go` defines `Outcome`, `Disposition`, `ParkReason`, `ParkReasonValid()`
2. ✅ `ToOutcome` / `FromOutcome` adapters
3. ✅ `TestOutcome_RoundTripsExecuteBeadReport` (covers all 16 disposition/park combos)
4. ✅ `ParkReason` closed type with `String()` + `TestParkReasonVocabulary` enumerates all 11 valid values
5. ✅ `go test ./internal/agent/try/` green
6. ✅ No callers — full repo build passes; only the new package references the type
